
  Usage: git-wrangler remove-secrets --confirm
  Description: Permanently purges sensitive files from the entire Git history
  Category: History Rewriting

    Permanently purges files containing sensitive data from the entire Git
    history of all managed repositories (across all branches and tags).
    Scans for common secret file patterns (.env, *.pem, *.key, id_rsa, etc.)
    and removes any matches using git-filter-repo. Requires 'git-filter-repo'
    to be installed. The remote origin URL is automatically restored after
    the rewrite.

    The list of target patterns is hardcoded in the script. --confirm is
    required before any destructive history rewrite is performed.


  Options:
      --confirm  (required) Confirm noninteractive history rewriting.


  Example:
      git-wrangler remove-secrets --confirm
